design_decisions.handle_rights_status_types: All in the new SAFE module crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs (#![forbid(unsafe_code)], 100% safe Rust, the largest pure-safe subsystem). Handle(u64) #[repr(transparent)]: layout (generation:u32)<<32 | slot:u32; NULL=Handle(0) reserved-invalid (gen 0 never issued, so a zeroed register can never resolve); new(gen,slot)/from_raw(v)/raw()/generation()/slot(). Authority lives in the SLOT, never the handle, so a forged u64 hits the wrong generation (Stale) or a vacant slot (BadCap/Stale) -- it can never manufacture rights (I5 unforgeability). Rights(u32) #[repr(transparent)]: 12 named bits READ/WRITE/TRANSFER/DUP/REVOKE + agent-semantic INVOKE_MODEL/SPAWN_AGENT/WRITE_PROCEDURAL/RECALL/CONSOLIDATE/EMIT_EXTERNAL/DELEGATE_BUDGET + NONE. The ONLY rights-narrowing primitive is intersect (bitwise AND) -> monotonic attenuation by construction; union assembles authority already held; contains is the dispatch gate; is_subset_of is the proof predicate; from_bits/bits round-trip a NARROW mask. SysStatus #[repr(u32)] CLOSED enum Ok=0/BadCap/BadMethod/Denied/Stale/WouldBlock/NoMem/ObjFull -- a total Rust enum, never a negative errno (an unrepresentable error is a compile error). ObjKind #[repr(u32)] closed enum took gen:robustness's RICHER forward-compat set (Agent/ModelSession/MemoryHome/MemoryRecord/Budget/Channel/Skill/Namespace/Generic) so M12-M17 add no ObjKind ABI churn; identity-only at M11, `o.kind as u64` is the inspect return.
design_decisions.object_registry: Per-principal HandleTable { slots: Vec<Slot>, free: Vec<u32>, cap: usize }; Slot { generation:u32, rights:Rights, object:Option<Rc<Object>> }. Object { kind:ObjKind } held behind alloc::rc::Rc so an object lives while any handle references it and is freed when the last handle is revoked/closed/transferred-out (Zircon refcount). Occupancy = object.is_some() and is checked INDEPENDENTLY of the generation match in live(), so a forged handle whose generation happens to equal a vacant slot's can never resolve Ok. Heap-backed (M5/M7 allocator -- tb-hal now declares `extern crate alloc;`, resolved by the kernel binary's #[global_allocator] in heap.rs; cargo kbuild passes -Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc). `cap` bounds the table fail-closed (mint->None == ObjFull). A LIFO free list reuses revoked slots whose generation a prior revoke already advanced. The whole module uses only Vec/Rc, zero unsafe.
design_decisions.dispatcher_abi: SyscallArgs #[repr(C)] { method:u32, handle:Handle, args:[u64;4] } (pointer-free at M11; buffers arrive with M14 channels) is the neutral descriptor the register-lift shim builds. SysReturn { status:SysStatus, value:u64 } (one typed scalar out -- never fd/errno/path). pub fn dispatch(&mut HandleTable, &SyscallArgs)->SysReturn is THE single audit chokepoint: (1) required_right(method) None=>BadMethod (closed method set); (2) table.live(handle) BadCap (out of range) / Stale (vacant or gen mismatch); (3) slot.rights.contains(need) ->Denied (the single confused-deputy gate -- the cap is resolved against the CALLER's table, never an ambient one); (4) run the typed method -> closed result. Method set (sparse, forward-compat): meta-ops [0,16) M_OBJECT_INSPECT(0,READ)/M_HANDLE_DUP(1,DUP)/M_HANDLE_NARROW(2,NONE)/M_HANDLE_TRANSFER(3,TRANSFER)/M_HANDLE_REVOKE(4,REVOKE)/M_HANDLE_CLOSE(5,NONE); agent verbs [16,..) M_AGENT_SPAWN(16)/M_MODEL_INVOKE(17)/M_MEM_WRITE_PROC(18)/M_MEM_RECALL(19)/M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE(20)/M_EMIT_EXTERNAL(21)/M_BUDGET_DELEGATE(22) -- rights-checked Ok stubs at M11; bodies land M12+ as NEW method bodies, not new ABI. Mirrors the existing M1 set_trap_hook/dispatch_trap and M8 set_irq_hook/dispatch_irq seams. INBOUND boundary proven now; returning the SysStatus into the ring3/EL0 result register is the explicit M12 generalization of the M9 preemption path.
design_decisions.revocation_model: DECIDED HERE per ROADMAP-V2 §3: v2 ships PER-SLOT GENERATION REVOKE (mandatory, proven, O(1)). HandleTable::free_slot drops the object (occupancy off) and bumps the slot generation via checked_add -- RETIRE on overflow (the slot is never returned to the free list, never silently wrapped, closing the resurrected-stale-handle forgery vector). Every extant handle to a revoked slot then resolves Stale in O(1) (no scan, no CDT walk). revoke() is the MECHANISM (no rights re-check -- the REVOKE-right gate is enforced for unprivileged callers by dispatch via required_right(M_HANDLE_REVOKE)=REVOKE). Soundness conditions made explicit in code: (a) generation bumped on free BEFORE reuse (no ABA); (b) occupancy checked independently of the generation match; (c) retire-on-overflow; (d) Handle(0) reserved NULL. Deferred refinements documented in caps.rs and ROADMAP §3 (the u64 Handle layout stays forward-compatible, no ABI break, land at M14): an O(1) per-object epoch (kill-the-object-everywhere) and the full seL4 Capability Derivation Tree for partial sub-tree revoke (v3).
design_decisions.register_lift_shim: The ONLY new unsafe in M11, confined to tb-hal arch/. BOTH arches keep every M4 artifact byte-for-byte intact (grafted gen:robustness's isolation on x86; symmetric fresh-window on aarch64). x86_64 (arch/x86_64/user.rs): a 2nd PIC ring3 stub (caps_user_stub) loads rax=method, rdi=handle (`mov edi,1; shl rdi,32` -> 1<<32, no imm64 relocation), rsi/rdx=0 and traps via a FRESH DPL=3 vector `int 0x81` (CAPS_SYSCALL_VECTOR=0x81; M4 keeps 0x80 fully intact) into a SEPARATE cap code page mapped as a new PT[2] leaf at CAPS_CODE_VA=USER_CODE_VA+0x2000 under the existing M4 PML4[1]->PDPT[0]->PD[0] chain (first-map+invlpg; the M4 USER_CODE page is NOT overwritten, removing the SMC/iretq-serialization assumption). caps_syscall_entry (#[unsafe(naked)], the only new x86 unsafe) marshals (rax,rdi,rsi,rdx)->SysV (rdi,rsi,rdx,rcx), sub rsp,8, calls the safe recorder caps_x86_syscall_shim (5 arch-local atomics), then takes the SAME KERNEL_RESUME_SP longjmp as M4 syscall_entry. aarch64 (arch/aarch64/user.rs): a 2nd movz/movk PIC EL0 stub (caps_user_stub) loads x8=method, x0=handle (movk x0,#1,lsl#32), x1/x2=0, svc #0; a FRESH EL0 window at the VACANT L1[5] (VA 5 GiB = 0x1_4000_0000) -- CRITICAL FIX vs the candidate, which used L1[4]: on aarch64 L1[4] is the M7 HEAP window (HEAP_WINDOW_BASE=0x1_0000_0000) and would clobber the heap the cap table lives on; L1[5] is verified vacant tree-wide, leaving the M4 L1[3] AND heap L1[4] untouched, first-map so no Break-Before-Make/TLBI. The EXISTING aarch64_el0_sync_handler is extended by NESTING `if CAPS_PROBE {lift x8/x0/x1/x2 into arch atomics} else {M4 single-arg path}` INSIDE the existing `if ec==EC_SVC64 {..} else {unexpected}` -- the M4 svc path is byte-identical when CAPS_PROBE is clear (its default), the outer unexpected-EL0 branch is untouched. Capture state is per-arch (5 atomics next to each shim), not lib.rs-resident; the thin lib.rs facade caps_user_probe(root_handle) forwards to arch::caps_user_probe, mirroring the user_demo facade. NO table/rights/generation/comparison logic touches asm.
design_decisions.principal_model: A principal == one HandleTable (the unit of authority; Zircon process-local handles -- naming a cap you do not hold is impossible: a forged index resolves Stale/BadCap and rights live server-side so it cannot amplify). A principal is born with a bootstrap root capability; the M11 self-test mints ObjKind::Agent with a broad rights set as the deterministic FIRST cap (generation 1, slot 0 = 0x0000_0001_0000_0000), which the PIC ring3/EL0 stub carries as an immediate. Zero ambient authority: no global fd table, no path namespace, no errno, no ioctl. The only way to obtain a NEW rights set is mint-from-an-authority-you-hold; the only way to weaken is intersect; the only way to move is transfer_to (re-mint in the receiver with a fresh slot+generation+same Rc<Object>, then vacate the sender -- the raw u64 is never reused across tables). dispatch resolves args.handle against the CALLER's OWN table, never an ambient/global one (confused-deputy stop; absence of the right -> Denied). DEFENSIVE: the per-arch caps_user_probe(root_handle) guard returns None unless root_handle == 0x0000_0001_0000_0000, AND the kernel asserts lifted.handle == h_root and fails closed (M11: FAIL, no marker) -- two independent checks on the stub-constant/first-mint coupling. Birth-set/manifest-prefix confinement (Fuchsia birth protocol) is the I3 bound noted for M12 spawn.
design_decisions.selftest_plan: In kernel/src/main.rs rust_main, AFTER the unchanged 'M10: addrspace OK' marker and BEFORE the final halt (zero unsafe blocks; single-vCPU, interrupts masked -- the timer was disarmed before M9's marker; the M10 self-test left the live root = default boot root, where M4's PML4[1]/L1[3] user window is present): (1) heap-backed caps::HandleTable::with_capacity(8); mint the broad root cap FIRST so its handle is the deterministic 0x0000_0001_0000_0000 the stub names by const; m11_fail(why) prints 'M11: FAIL <why>' + halts (no marker) so the runner grep fails loudly. (2) ABI proof: tb_hal::caps_user_probe(h_root.raw()) drives ONE real ring3/EL0 numbered syscall through the register-lift shim; assert lifted.method==M_OBJECT_INSPECT && lifted.handle==h_root, then caps::dispatch(&mut root,&lifted).status==Ok -- a capability-checked unprivileged call with no fd/errno crossing the boundary; prints 'caps: ring3 numbered dispatcher returned Ok'. (3) the rights-mask invariant kernel-side in safe Rust (the proof matrix, retained verbatim from gen:correctness): unknown method 0xDEAD->BadMethod; handle naming no slot (0x0000_0001_0000_0007)->BadCap; M_MEM_WRITE_PROC on a READ-only cap->Denied (confused-deputy stop); narrow(root,READ) is_subset_of root rights (attenuation never widens); mint+revoke victim then dispatch->Stale (O(1) UAR); transfer_to a child table -> source Stale, dest Ok (move semantics); capacity-1 table second mint->ObjFull (closed status, no panic); prints 'caps: attenuate/transfer/revoke/ObjFull algebra holds'. Then prints exactly 'M11: caps OK\n', then tb_hal::halt() LAST. Build arbiter: cargo kbuild --target targets/{x86_64,aarch64}-tabos-none.json, 0 warnings (#![deny(missing_docs)] satisfied -- every pub item in caps.rs + the lib.rs facade is documented; arch additions are under the private mod arch so missing_docs does not apply), boot to 'M11: caps OK' under QEMU x86_64 microvm + aarch64 virt + tb-vmm; all three run scripts + bench-boot.sh advanced to the M11 marker. Off-target proof harness: caps.rs is pointer-free pure-safe Rust, Kani/host-test-targetable for I1 attenuation (narrow subset), I2 no-amplify, I4 UAR-soundness (post-revoke never Ok), I5 unforgeability.

new_files.content:
{
  "marker": "M11: caps OK",
  "milestone": "M11 - capability handle table + kernel object model + agent-native non-POSIX syscall ABI",
  "base": "gen:correctness (unanimous across 3 reviews), with gen:robustness grafts applied",
  "grafts_applied": [
    "x86_64 register-lift isolation: FRESH DPL=3 vector int 0x81 (CAPS_SYSCALL_VECTOR) + SEPARATE cap code page mapped as a new PT[2] leaf at USER_CODE_VA+0x2000 under the M4 PML4[1]->PDPT[0]->PD[0] chain (first-map+invlpg). The live M4 int-0x80 gate and the M4 USER_CODE page are left byte-for-byte intact, removing the self-modifying-code / iretq-serialization assumption.",
    "Thin lib.rs facade caps_user_probe(root_handle: u64) -> Option<caps::SyscallArgs> forwarding to arch::caps_user_probe; the 5 register-capture atomics live PER-ARCH next to each shim (not lib.rs-resident), mirroring the user_demo facade.",
    "Fail-closed defensive guard: each arch caps_user_probe returns None unless root_handle == 0x0000_0001_0000_0000, in addition to the kernel-side assert lifted.handle == h_root.",
    "Richer ObjKind set (Agent/ModelSession/MemoryHome/MemoryRecord/Budget/Channel/Skill/Namespace/Generic) for M12-M17 forward-compat; identity-only at M11."
  ],
  "corrections_over_candidate": [
    "CRITICAL aarch64 fix: the candidate's fresh EL0 window used L1[4] (VA 0x1_0000_0000), but on aarch64 L1[4] IS the M7 heap window (HEAP_WINDOW_BASE = 0x1_0000_0000 in arch/aarch64/mmu.rs). Writing it would clobber the heap the cap HandleTable lives on and crash the self-test. The window is moved to the VACANT L1[5] (VA 0x1_4000_0000), verified unused tree-wide; M4's L1[3] and the heap's L1[4] are both untouched."
  ],
  "design_decisions": {
    "handle_rights_status_types": "Handle(u64)=(generation:u32)<<32|slot; NULL=gen0 reserved-invalid. Rights(u32) 12-bit bitset, intersect (bitwise AND) the only narrowing primitive (monotone). SysStatus #[repr(u32)] CLOSED enum Ok/BadCap/BadMethod/Denied/Stale/WouldBlock/NoMem/ObjFull. ObjKind #[repr(u32)] 9-variant forward-compat set. All in crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs under #![forbid(unsafe_code)].",
    "object_registry": "HandleTable{slots:Vec<Slot>,free:Vec<u32>,cap}; Slot{generation,rights,object:Option<Rc<Object>>}; Object{kind:ObjKind} behind Rc (refcount lifetime). Occupancy = object.is_some(), checked independently of the generation match. Heap-backed via tb-hal `extern crate alloc;` (kernel binary provides the #[global_allocator]).",
    "dispatcher_abi": "SyscallArgs{method:u32,handle:Handle,args:[u64;4]} -> dispatch(&mut HandleTable,&SyscallArgs) -> SysReturn{status:SysStatus,value:u64}. Pipeline: required_right (BadMethod) -> live (BadCap/Stale) -> contains (Denied) -> typed method. Meta-ops [0,16); agent verbs [16,..) as rights-checked Ok stubs.",
    "revocation_model": "v2 per-slot generation revoke with retire-on-overflow (checked_add; overflowed slot retired, never reissued). O(1) use-after-revoke -> Stale, no ABA. Per-object epoch + seL4 CDT noted refinements for M14/v3; u64 Handle layout forward-compatible (no ABI break).",
    "register_lift_shim": "x86_64: 2nd PIC ring3 stub + int 0x81 (DPL=3) into a fresh PT[2] cap code page; caps_syscall_entry naked shim marshals rax/rdi/rsi/rdx -> SysV, calls safe recorder (arch atomics), SAME KERNEL_RESUME_SP longjmp; M4 0x80/USER_CODE untouched. aarch64: 2nd movz/movk EL0 stub + svc #0; fresh EL0 window at the VACANT L1[5] (VA 5 GiB); the existing EL0 sync handler nests `if CAPS_PROBE {lift x8/x0/x1/x2 into arch atomics} else {M4}` inside `if ec==EC_SVC64`; M4 path byte-identical when CAPS_PROBE clear. The ONLY new unsafe in M11.",
    "principal_model": "One HandleTable == one principal. Bootstrap root cap is the deterministic first mint (gen 1, slot 0 = 0x0000_0001_0000_0000) carried by the PIC stub as an immediate. dispatch resolves against the CALLER's own table (confused-deputy stop). transfer_to re-mints in the receiver + vacates the sender (never reuse the raw u64 across tables). Zero ambient authority.",
    "selftest_plan": "kernel/src/main.rs: mint root FIRST; tb_hal::caps_user_probe(h_root.raw()) drives ONE real ring3/EL0 numbered syscall through the lift shim -> dispatch Ok; then kernel-side algebra matrix BadMethod/BadCap/Denied/attenuate-subset/revoke->Stale/transfer-is-move/ObjFull, each fail-closed via m11_fail (prints 'M11: FAIL <why>', no marker). Prints exactly 'M11: caps OK\\n', then halt LAST."
  },
  "new_files": [
    {"path": "crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs", "role": "the entire SAFE capability subsystem: Handle/Rights/SysStatus/ObjKind/Object/SyscallArgs/SysReturn, the per-principal HandleTable (mint/live/alloc/free_slot/dup/narrow/revoke/close/transfer_to), the closed numbered method set + required_right, and the ONE numbered dispatch(); #![forbid(unsafe_code)]."}

    {"path": "scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh", "summary": "MARKER -> 'M11: caps OK'."},
    {"path": "scripts/bench-boot.sh", "summary": "FINAL_MARKER -> 'M11: caps OK'."},
    {"path": "docs/ROADMAP-V2.md", "summary": "§6 flip M11 -> complete (CI-green both arches + tb-vmm), M12 -> in progress, M13-M18 planned; §3 append the M11 'as shipped' note (per-slot generation revoke + retire-on-overflow + per-arch register-lift shim + INBOUND-only boundary -> M12 returns status to user frame)."}
  ],
  "selftest": "cargo kbuild --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json and ...aarch64-tabos-none.json, 0 warnings; boot to 'M11: caps OK' under QEMU x86_64 microvm + aarch64 virt + tb-vmm. All M2..M10 markers (incl 'M3: mmu OK', 'M4: user/ring OK') still print.",
  "build_notes": "tb-hal is edition 2021; `extern crate alloc;` resolves because cargo kbuild passes -Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc and the kernel binary owns the #[global_allocator]. caps.rs #![forbid(unsafe_code)] is genuine (Vec/Rc only). #![deny(missing_docs)] satisfied for all caps.rs pub items + the lib.rs facade; arch additions are under the private `mod arch` so missing_docs does not apply. CRITICAL: aarch64 M11 EL0 window MUST avoid L1[4] (the M7 heap); uses the vacant L1[5]."
}

patches.old_string:
v2 ships per-slot generation revoke; transitive/recursive revoke (seL4 CDT) is a
noted refinement.
patches.new_string:
v2 ships per-slot generation revoke; transitive/recursive revoke (seL4 CDT) is a
noted refinement.

*As shipped:* the revocation model is **per-slot generation revoke with
retire-on-overflow** — a slot whose `u32` generation would wrap is retired,
never reissued, closing the resurrected-stale-handle vector. The per-object
epoch ("kill the object in every table at once") and the seL4 CDT subtree revoke
are noted refinements the `u64` `Handle` layout stays forward-compatible with
(they land at M14 with no ABI break). The only new unsafe is the per-arch
register-lift shim: x86_64 routes the numbered cap syscall through a FRESH DPL=3
`int 0x81` gate into a SEPARATE `PT[2]` cap code page, and aarch64 maps a FRESH
EL0 window at the vacant `L1[5]` gated on a `CAPS_PROBE` flag — both leave the M4
`int 0x80` / EL0 `svc` path (and the aarch64 M7 heap window at `L1[4]`)
byte-for-byte intact, so `M4: user/ring OK` and the heap-backed cap table cannot
regress. The entire handle table, rights algebra, object registry and dispatch
live in `crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs` under `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`. M11 proves
the INBOUND boundary (a numbered, capability-checked syscall reaches `dispatch`
and yields a closed `SysStatus` kernel-side); returning that status into the
ring3/EL0 result register is the explicit M12 generalisation.

patches.old_string:
| **M11** (capability handle table + object model + syscall ABI) | ⏳ **in progress** (next increment) |
| M12 – M18 | ⬜ planned (this document) |
patches.new_string:
| **M11** (capability handle table + object model + syscall ABI) | ✅ **complete**, CI-green (x86_64 microvm + aarch64 virt + tb-vmm/`/dev/kvm`) — per-principal `HandleTable` with `Handle = (generation:u32)<<32 \| slot`, generation-checked O(1) resolve→`Stale`, the 12-bit `Rights` bitset whose only narrowing primitive is intersect (monotonic attenuation), the closed `SysStatus` enum, an `Rc`-counted object registry, and ONE numbered capability-checked `dispatch`er — all SAFE Rust in `caps.rs` (`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`); the only new unsafe is a per-arch register-lift shim (x86_64 fresh DPL=3 `int 0x81` + a new `PT[2]` cap code page; aarch64 fresh EL0 window at the vacant `L1[5]` gated on `CAPS_PROBE`) — the M4 `int 0x80`/EL0 path and the aarch64 heap window (`L1[4]`) left byte-for-byte intact; per-slot generation revoke (retire-on-overflow), seL4 CDT noted for v3 |
| **M12** (agent runtime — `AgentProcess`) | ⏳ **in progress** (next increment) |
| M13 – M18 | ⬜ planned (this document) |

selftest: Build arbiter (repo root, MINGW64): `cargo kbuild --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json` and `cargo kbuild --target targets/aarch64-tabos-none.json` -- both compile with 0 warnings. #![deny(missing_docs)] is satisfied (every pub item in caps.rs and the lib.rs caps_user_probe facade is documented; the arch user.rs additions live under the PRIVATE `mod arch`, so missing_docs does not apply to them); tb-hal's new `extern crate alloc;` resolves because kbuild passes -Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc and the kernel binary owns the #[global_allocator]. Boot: `bash scripts/run-x86_64.sh` (QEMU x86_64 microvm), `bash scripts/run-aarch64.sh` (QEMU aarch64 virt), `bash scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh` (tb-vmm via tb-boot v0). Each must print, in order, every prior marker incl. "M3: mmu OK", "M4: user/ring OK", ... "M10: addrspace OK", then the two `caps: ...` progress lines, then EXACTLY "M11: caps OK", then park (halt runs last). All four scripts now grep for the M11 marker. Boot-relevant proof points exercised live: a REAL ring3/EL0 numbered syscall is lifted by the register-lift shim (x86 int 0x81 into a fresh PT[2] page; aarch64 svc into a fresh L1[5] EL0 window) and dispatched to Ok; then BadMethod/BadCap/Denied(confused-deputy)/attenuate-subset/revoke->Stale(O(1) UAR)/transfer-is-move(source Stale,dest Ok)/ObjFull. Any failed invariant prints "M11: FAIL <why>" and halts BEFORE the marker so the grep fails loudly. Off-target proof harness (optional): caps.rs is pointer-free pure-safe Rust, so a host cargo test / Kani target over the same module discharges I1 (narrow subset), I2 (no amplification), I4 (post-revoke never Ok), I5 (forged handle never Ok with rights).
build_notes: 1) tb-hal is edition 2021 (NOT 2024). `extern crate alloc;` is the one line that names alloc in tb-hal; it links because `cargo kbuild` already builds `-Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc` and the kernel binary provides the `#[global_allocator]` (heap.rs). No allocator is defined in tb-hal; tb-vmm (separate std workspace) does not link tb-hal, so the host build is unaffected. 2) caps.rs places `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` as the first line, followed by the `//!` module docs -- both are inner attributes, order-independent, valid. 3) `#![deny(missing_docs)]` is crate-wide on tb-hal: EVERY pub item in caps.rs (Handle/Rights/SysStatus/ObjKind/Object/SyscallArgs/SysReturn + all variants/fields/methods/method-consts) and the lib.rs facade carries a doc comment. The arch/x86_64|aarch64/user.rs additions are under the PRIVATE `mod arch` (lib.rs line 33), so missing_docs does NOT apply to their pub fns -- matching the existing user.rs which already mixes `///` and `//` and compiles clean. 4) CRITICAL aarch64 fix vs the candidate: the fresh EL0 window must NOT use L1[4] -- on aarch64 L1[4] (VA 0x1_0000_0000) IS the M7 heap window (`HEAP_WINDOW_BASE` in arch/aarch64/mmu.rs), and writing it would clobber the heap the cap HandleTable lives on, crashing the self-test. The window is at the VACANT L1[5] (VA 0x1_4000_0000), verified unused tree-wide; a `const _: () = assert!(CAPS_USER_L1_IDX == 5)` locks it. 5) x86 isolation: fresh DPL=3 vector 0x81 (set_user_gate debug_assert!(vector<256) holds; 0x81=129) + a new PT[2] leaf at USER_CODE_VA+0x2000 under the M4 PML4[1] chain -- does NOT touch the M4 0x80 gate, the M4 USER_CODE page, the heap (PML4[2]) or the LAPIC (PML4[3]). 6) The two M11 global_asm! stubs share the `.text.user` section; the x86 cap stub uses the numeric label `3:` (M4 uses `2:`) to avoid an assembler duplicate-label error; aarch64 stubs are separate naked-fn asm blocks so `1:` does not clash. 7) naked shims are `#[unsafe(naked)] unsafe extern "C"` + `naked_asm!` (matches M4's syscall_entry/enter_ring3) -- no inner unsafe block needed; the `unsafe fn map_caps_code` keeps an inner `unsafe {}` block exactly like the existing `unsafe fn map_user_pages`, so no unused_unsafe/unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn churn. 8) caps_syscall_entry's `sub rsp,8` re-establishes SysV 16-alignment after the CPU's 5-qword push (rsp%16==8 -> after sub ==0 -> after call ==8), identical to M4. 9) dead_code: `caps` is a `pub mod`, so every pub item is public API (no dead_code on unused methods like dup/close); caps_syscall_entry/caps_x86_syscall_shim are reached via `sym`, caps_user_stub via `as *const ()`, caps_user_probe via the arch/mod re-export. 10) The kernel M11 block imports exactly `{self, Handle, ObjKind, Rights, SyscallArgs, SysStatus}` and uses all of them -- no unused-import warning; it contains ZERO `unsafe {}` blocks (only safe tb_hal/caps calls), preserving the kernel-crate invariant. 11) `o.kind as u64` in dispatch is valid because ObjKind is `#[repr(u32)]`. 12) The probe runs under the default boot root (M10 switched back), where M4's PML4[1]/L1[3] user window is present; map_user_pages is idempotent on re-call.
